pi_items.source: Verified against export_result.sh checks for EXPECTED_FILE and task description instructions.
Verified against task description and verifier.py checking for 'denver' in location.
Verified against task description instructions and verifier.py logic (7.8 <= sys_size <= 8.2).
Verified against task description instructions and verifier.py logic (34 <= tilt <= 36).
Verified against task description instructions and verifier.py logic (175 <= az <= 185).
Verified against task description ($0.08/kWh) and verifier.py logic (7.8 <= ppa_price <= 8.2).
Verified against task description (2.0%) and verifier.py logic (1.9 <= ppa_esc <= 2.1).
Contradicted by verifier.py logic, which enforces a minimum capacity factor of 13.0%, allowing an annual energy of ~9110.4 kWh for an 8.0 kW system.
Contradicted by verifier.py logic, which enforces a maximum capacity factor of 21.0%, allowing an annual energy up to ~14716.8 kWh for an 8.0 kW system.
Contradicted by verifier.py logic, which explicitly uses 'cf_min = 13.0' for its physics sanity check.
Verified against verifier.py logic which explicitly uses 'cf_max = 21.0'.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires the agent to generate a synthetic proposal for a residential solar lease in Denver using PySAM. Most configuration parameters from the task description match the verifier's assertions (e.g., 8.0 kW DC size, 35 deg tilt, 180 deg azimuth, $0.08/kWh PPA price). However, the metadata claims for expected minimum/maximum annual energy (10,000 to 14,000 kWh) and minimum capacity factor (14%) are explicitly contradicted by the verifier logic, which strictly enforces a wider capacity factor range of 13.0% to 21.0% (translating to a wider annual energy range of approximately 9110.4 to 14716.8 kWh for an 8.0 kW system).
